heading: Reports and audits
label: 220511 Reports and audits

Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Not later than June 1 of each year, the corporation shall submit simultaneously to the President and to each House of Congress a detailed report of its operations during the prior calendar year, including—Pub. L. 105–277“(1) a complete statement of the corporation’s receipts and expenditures; and“(2) a comprehensive description of the activities and accomplishments of the corporation during the prior year.”

children.heading: .—Submission to president and congress
children.label: 1 .—Submission to president and congress
children.text: Not less frequently than annually, the corporation shall submit simultaneously to the President and to each House of Congress a detailed report on the operations of the corporation for the preceding calendar year.

children.label: A
children.text: A comprehensive description of the activities and accomplishments of the corporation during such calendar year.

children.label: B
children.text: Data concerning the participation of women, disabled individuals, and racial and ethnic minorities in the amateur athletic activities and administration of the corporation and national governing bodies.

children.label: C
children.text: A description of the steps taken to encourage the participation of women, disabled individuals, and racial minorities in amateur athletic activities.

children.label: D
children.text: A description of any lawsuit or grievance filed against the corporation, including any dispute initiated under this chapter.

children.label: E
children.text: The agenda and minutes of any meeting of the board of directors of the corporation that occurred during such calendar year.

children.label: I
children.text: the areas in which the corporation has met compliance standards; and

children.label: II
children.text: the areas in which the corporation has not met compliance standards; and

children.label: ii
children.text: assesses the compliance of each member of the corporation and provides a plan for improvement, as necessary.

children.label: G
children.text: A detailed description of any complaint of retaliation made during such calendar year, including the entity involved, the number of allegations of retaliation, and the outcome of such allegations.

children.heading: .—Public availability
children.label: 3 .—Public availability
children.text: The corporation shall make each report under this subsection available to the public on an easily accessible internet website of the corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: an independent certified public accountant; or

children.label: B
children.text: an independent licensed public accountant who is certified or licensed by the regulatory authority of a State or a political subdivision of a State.

children.heading: .—Location
children.label: 2 .—Location
children.text: An audit under paragraph (1) shall be conducted at the location at which the financial statements of the corporation normally are kept.

children.label: A
children.text: all records and property owned or used by the corporation, as necessary to facilitate the audit; and

children.label: B
children.text: any facility under audit for the purpose of verifying transactions, including any balance or security held by a depository, fiscal agent, or custodian.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the end of the fiscal year for which an audit is carried out, the auditor shall submit a report on the audit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate, the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives, and the chair of the Athletes’ Advisory Council.

children.label: i
children.text: Any statement necessary to present fairly the assets, liabilities, and surplus or deficit of the corporation.

children.label: ii
children.text: An analysis of the changes in the amounts of such assets, liabilities, and surplus or deficit.

children.label: iii
children.text: A detailed statement of the income and expenses of the corporation, including the results of any trading, manufacturing, publishing, or other commercial endeavor.

children.label: iv
children.text: A detailed statement of the amounts spent on stipends and services for athletes.

children.label: v
children.text: A detailed statement of the amounts spent on compensation and services for executives and administration officials of the corporation, including the 20 employees of the corporation who receive the highest amounts of compensation.

children.label: vi
children.text: A detailed statement of the amounts allocated to the national governing bodies.

children.label: vii
children.text: Such comments and information as the auditor considers necessary to inform Congress of the financial operations and condition of the corporation.

children.label: viii
children.text: Recommendations relating to the financial operations and condition of the corporation.

children.label: I
children.text: a member of the board of directors of the corporation; or

children.label: II
children.text: any senior management personnel of the corporation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The corporation shall make each report under this paragraph available to the public on an easily accessible internet website of the corporation.

children.heading: .—Personally identifiable information
children.label: ii .—Personally identifiable information
children.text: A report made available under clause (i) shall not include the personally identifiable information of any individual.
